response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer, but his profile does not match this role's core requirements. His C++ experience is limited to a short stint at National Instruments (~6 months in 2014–2015) and a thesis project, far from the 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ demanded. His database background is MongoDB/MySQL rather than SQL Server/Oracle, and his financial exposure (RIDE Capital) is retail investment fintech, not fixed income, derivatives, or capital-markets systems. While he brings excellent engineering practices, low-latency systems experience, and CI/CD knowledge, the mandatory skill gaps are too large for this consulting engagement.
strengths: Extensive senior-level engineering experience with strong craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Broad polyglot background (C/C++, D, Java, TypeScript) with proven ability to learn new stacks quickly
concerns: Only ~6 months of professional C++ experience, and none on Windows-focused development in the required depth
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database work centers on MongoDB and MySQL
No fixed income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain experience; fintech exposure is limited to a retail investment platform

key_factors.factor: Missing fixed income / capital-markets domain expertise
Strong general engineering and low-latency systems background
